Project.Name: EquiFlow: The Self-Healing AI Governance Engine

Scope.Mission: To build an open-source, agentic protocol that audits AI models and datasets for hidden biases, translates raw statistical disparities into human-readable narratives, and provides an automated, self-healing correction loop.

Feature.name: Dual-Mode Ingestion
Feature.Detail: White-Box: Accepts raw training datasets (CSV/SQL) to detect proxy variables and representation bias before a model is trained.
Black-Box: Accepts API endpoints of black-box models to conduct adversarial testing using synthetic data generation.

Features.Feature: Utilizes LLMs to generate thousands of synthetic "Ghost Personas" with controlled demographic variations to stress-test target models and record disparate impacts.
Converts standard statistical parity metrics (like disparate impact ratios) into plain-English severity reports, explaining who is being harmed and why.

Feature.name: Auto-Mitigation Loop
Feature.Detail: Generates and injects synthetic profiles to balance skewed datasets.
Outputs dynamic inference-time guardrail prompts to correct live AI behavior.

Features.Feature: Exposes the entire engine as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so external developers and enterprise systems can run fairness checks natively within their existing workflows.

ExecutionPhases.Phase: Start by writing the Python scripts that can take a local CSV, run the AIF360 math, and use Gemini to generate the plain-English report.
Wrap the core logic in a FastAPI service and hook it up to PostgreSQL to save the audit results.
Build the Next.js UI where a user can upload a file, hit "Run Audit", and view the generated EquiLens dashboard.
Finally, expose the established API endpoints through an MCP server so it functions as a global plug-and-play tool.
